channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Cargo plane goes missing off Pakistan's coast
item.description: A cargo plane with five crew members aboard went missing off the Pakistani port of Karachi late Tuesday after rapidly descending and losing contact with air traffic controllers on a flight from the United Arab Emirates, officials said. Pakistan’s airport authority posted on X that search and Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 30 Pakistan Coast Guards personnel killed in suicide bombing
item.description: The Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) has claimed responsibility for a major attack on a Pakistan Coast Guards camp in the Panwan area of Jiwani, Gwadar district, alleging that more than 30 security personnel were killed and dozens injured in what it described as a "fidayeen" (suicide) operation. In a Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Mamata faces fresh blow as new TMC Bengal chief Chandrima quits
item.description: Amid continued setbacks and rebellion involving Trinamool Congress (TMC) since the last assembly election, the party chief in the Indian state of West Bengal has resigned. Chandrima Bhattacharya, a former minister and three-time MLA in West Bengal and long considered one of the party chief Mamata Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Iran's slain leader Khamenei laid in state in Tehran for week of mass funeral events
item.description: The body of Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was lying in state in a vast hall in Tehran on Friday as clerics, officials, foreign dignitaries and other mourners paid their respects after his 37-year rule. Iran is staging a week of mass funeral processions for Khamenei -- killed i Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
item.description: Instagram has been running paid adverts promoting child sexual abuse material in India, a BBC Eye investigation has found.The ads, seen by the BBC World Service, use terms including "rape video" and "child video" and link users to channels on the messaging app Telegram, where they can buy the mater Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Sri Lanka mulls pharmacy sale of drug test kits
item.description: Sri Lanka is considering introducing drug test kits through pharmacies to allow parents to privately check whether their children are using narcotics, the President's Media Division (PMD) said. The proposal was discussed when the national council of the "A Nation United" anti-drug campaign, launch Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Forty killed as bus falls into ditch in Pakistan
item.description: Forty people ​were killed and ‌eight others injured after a bus ​fell into ​a deep roadside ⁠ditch in Pakistan's ​southwestern province ​of Balochistan on Friday, a rescue agency ​and a ​government official said. The bus ‌was ⁠headed to the national capital, Islamabad, from the provincia Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Powerful Iranian general emerges before Khamenei funeral
item.description: A powerful general who leads Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard emerged from hiding as Tehran prepared Friday for the dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Photos published online by Iranian state media showed Gen. Ahmad Vahidi attending a meeting about the fune Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Seven killed in India as bus hits trailer, goes up in flames
item.description: At least seven people have been killed and 22 others injured after a sleeper bus crashed into a trailer and caught on fire on the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in India’s Rajasthan, reports bdnews24.com citing NDTV. The incident took place around 2:30am local time on Wednesday when the bus w Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crash kills 14
item.description: A helicopter belonging to Saudi oil giant Aramco crashed on Sunday in Ras Tanura on Saudi Arabia's eastern coast on the Gulf, west of the Strait of Hormuz, killing 14 nationals, the state news agency reported, adding that the cause was unknown. Aramco had resumed crude oil loadings on Friday at it Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: Eight killed in Pakistani military operations in Balochistan
item.description: Pakistan’s military says that it has killed eight “terrorists” in intelligence operations in Balochistan’s Kharan and Mastung districts, reports Pakistani daily Dawn. The country’s Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) said it had conducted the “intelligence-dr Delivered by PolitePaul service

item.title: 700 civilians killed by Myanmar Army in 6 months
item.description: Myanmar's military killed more than 700 civilians in six months, including hundreds of women and children, according to a UN report cited by the BBC. The report from the UN Human Rights Office, covering August to January -- the six months from when the military announced elections -- verified a mi Delivered by PolitePaul service
